title: Bidens leucanthema
intro: Bidens leucanthema, also known as Bidens or Nannyplume, is a perennial herbaceous plant belonging to the Asteraceae family, native to Europe and Western Asia. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including respiratory and gastrointestinal problems, due to its potential anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Bidens leucanthema include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and essential oils, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Bidens leucanthema is commonly used to make herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to alleviate symptoms of colds, fever, and digestive issues.
taxonomy: Bidens leucanthema is a plant species in the family Asteraceae. It belongs to the genus Bidens, which is a group of annual and perennial plants commonly known as beggar-ticks or tickseed. Bidens leucanthema is native to Europe and is also found in other parts of the world. The plant is often found growing in damp or moist areas, including meadows and along streams. It is classified as a dicotyledonous plant, meaning it has two cotyledons or seed leaves when it germinates.
